_note: Ground truth for the Ema Software Engineering Lead ML JD (swe_jd_ema.txt). Updated after first eval run: 'data processing', 'data warehousing', 'large-scale data systems', 'software development', 'data structures', 'algorithms' are legitimately in the JD text and correctly extracted as required. Moved from false_positives to required_skills.

_annotation_notes.v1_false_positives_resolved: After first eval run, data processing / data warehousing / large-scale data systems / software development / data structures / algorithms were flagged as extractor false positives. Re-checked against JD text: all 6 appear explicitly ('data processing (SQL, ETL, data warehousing)', 'software development principles, data structures, and algorithms'). Moved to required_skills.
_annotation_notes.v1_missed_skills: The extractor missed: large language models, transformer models, feature engineering, model training, model deployment, A/B testing, MLOps, cloud platforms. Root cause: Ema JD uses 'Ideally, you'd have:' soft framing for all requirements. Extractor prompt treats this as preferred. Mitigation: add a prompt rule — 'For startup JDs using soft framing for all requirements, treat core ML technical skills as required regardless of framing.'
_annotation_notes.pytorch_vs_tensorflow: JD says 'TensorFlow or PyTorch' — both in required. LLM matcher should accept either as satisfying the requirement.
_annotation_notes.retrieval_ranking_rl_agents: JD says 'deep understanding of ANY of...' — required as a group, candidate needs depth in at least one.
_annotation_notes.preferred_simplified: Moved data warehousing and large-scale data systems to required after v1 review. GCP/Azure remain preferred (JD says 'like GCP or Azure' — specific providers are differentiators).
